reasoning: The informal claim "G ≅ Z_p for some prime p" is logically equivalent to "G is cyclic and G has prime cardinality". The Lean statement conjoins `IsCyclic G` with `∃ G_fin : Fintype G, Nat.Prime (@card G G_fin)`, which exactly asserts this: cyclicity plus finiteness with prime order. The existential over Fintype is effectively "G is finite" (any Fintype instance yields the same cardinality), and the prime cardinality ensures G ≅ Z_p. The hypotheses `[CommGroup G] [IsSimpleGroup G]` correctly encode "abelian simple group", and crucially `G : Type*` has no Fintype assumption a priori — matching the informal "do not assume G is a finite group". This is a faithful formalization.
